title: Lasiandra cardinalis
intro: Lasiandra cardinalis, also known as Cardinal's Hat or Cardinal's Beanie, is a flowering plant species native to South America, characterized by its distinctive red and white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Lasiandra cardinalis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Lasiandra cardinalis is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, which can be used to treat respiratory issues, colds, and flu, as well as to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
taxonomy: Lasiandra cardinalis is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to Brazil and belongs to the genus Lasiandra. The classification of Lasiandra cardinalis is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, clade Asterids, order Gentianales, family Rubiaceae, genus Lasiandra, species L. cardinalis. Lasiandra cardinalis is a member of the Rubiaceae family, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. The exact classification may vary slightly depending on the source, but Lasiandra cardinalis is generally classified as a species within the family Rubiaceae.
